text: I guess my opinion just doesn't matter to you at all.
boundary_note: Passive-aggressive phrasing masks anger at being disregarded, not sadness from loneliness.

text: They gave the promotion to the new guy after all my overtime. Just like that.
boundary_note: Focus is on the blatant injustice and lack of recognition, not personal inadequacy.

text: Why do I even bother trying so hard? It never changes anything.
boundary_note: Rhetorical question expresses exasperated fury at futility, not depressive resignation.

text: Of course the one file I needed is corrupted. Just my luck.
boundary_note: Sarcasm ('just my luck') indicates anger at the universe, not despair over loss.

text: So all those promises were just words to get you what you wanted.
boundary_note: The tone is accusatory and focused on betrayal, not mournful over lost trust.

text: He saw my message and just ignored it. I feel completely invisible.
boundary_note: Feeling 'invisible' here stems from anger at being disrespected, not sadness from neglect.

text: I can't believe I let myself get my hopes up again for this.
boundary_note: Self-criticism is rooted in frustration with a repeated mistake, not true sorrow.

text: They changed the rules halfway through the competition. How is that fair?
boundary_note: This is a direct challenge to injustice, expressing outrage, not sadness.

text: It’s fine. I'm used to being the one who has to cancel my plans.
boundary_note: The phrase 'It's fine' is dripping with sarcasm, a classic sign of suppressed rage.

text: After everything I did for them, they just cut me out completely.
boundary_note: The emotional core is betrayal and the unfairness of the outcome, not grief.

text: I really thought I deserved better than this. What a joke.
boundary_note: Calling the situation a 'joke' is a cynical expression of anger, not disappointment.

text: My flight was canceled after I waited at the airport for five hours.
boundary_note: The focus is on wasted time and frustration, not the loss of a trip.

text: You just stood there and let them say that about me without a word.
boundary_note: This is an accusation of disloyalty and cowardice, fueling anger, not hurt feelings.

text: She took credit for my idea in the meeting. I feel so small.
boundary_note: Feeling 'small' is a reaction to the injustice and powerlessness, sparking rage.

text: I poured my soul into that project and it gets completely ignored.
boundary_note: The energy is furious indignation over wasted effort, not sadness over failure.

text: The website crashed during checkout and I lost my spot in the queue.
boundary_note: This is pure situational frustration and rage at inefficiency, not sorrow.

text: Wow, so I'm just supposed to be okay with this decision you made?
boundary_note: 'Wow' and the rhetorical question signal incredulous anger at being railroaded.

text: They all went out and 'forgot' to invite me. Again.
boundary_note: The repetition 'Again' highlights a pattern of disrespect, which causes anger, not sadness.

text: I should have known better than to expect basic consideration.
boundary_note: This is cynical anger directed inward, a defense against the pain of disrespect.

text: This whole system feels designed to make you want to give up.
boundary_note: The statement critiques a hostile system, expressing fury at intentional obstruction.

text: I rearrange my whole day for you, and you cancel last minute.
boundary_note: The speaker is angry about their wasted effort and the lack of respect shown.

text: My own family didn't even bother to defend me. I feel so alone.
boundary_note: Feeling 'alone' is a symptom of the rage caused by deep family betrayal.

text: I bent over backwards for nothing. Absolutely nothing.
boundary_note: The repetition of 'nothing' emphasizes the fury over unreciprocated effort.

text: The one thing I asked for, and it was too much. Unbelievable.
boundary_note: 'Unbelievable' here conveys outraged disbelief, not sorrowful disappointment.

text: Don't tell me to calm down. You have no idea what this feels like.
boundary_note: A direct command born of anger at being invalidated, not a plea for sympathy.

text: He just threw away years of my work without a second thought.
boundary_note: The core emotion is outrage at the violation and disrespect for their labor.

text: I followed all the instructions perfectly and it still broke. What a waste.
boundary_note: The focus is on the unfairness of the outcome and anger at the wasted effort.

text: The warranty expired yesterday. Of course it did.
boundary_note: Sarcasm points to anger at a perceived cosmic injustice, not sadness over money.

text: I guess I was just convenient for you until something better came along.
boundary_note: This is a bitter accusation of being used, sparking anger and resentment.

text: She spread that rumor knowing it wasn't true. I feel so betrayed.
boundary_note: The feeling of betrayal is the direct cause of the speaker's intense fury.

text: I'm always the one who apologizes first. I'm just so tired.
boundary_note: Tiredness here is burnout from anger over a perceived injustice in a relationship.

text: I paid for premium service and this is the treatment I get?
boundary_note: This rhetorical question expresses indignation at a violation of a transactional agreement.

text: All that talk about loyalty, and this is what it amounts to.
boundary_note: A cynical and bitter observation fueled by anger from a deep betrayal.

text: They acted like my emergency was just an inconvenience for them.
boundary_note: The speaker is enraged by the lack of empathy and blatant selfishness.

text: Why do I let people treat me this way? It's my own fault.
boundary_note: Self-blame is a manifestation of anger and frustration at one's own passivity.

text: The bus drove right past me even though I was waving. I feel invisible.
boundary_note: Feeling 'invisible' is a reaction to being ignored, causing anger at the disrespect.

text: You knew how much this meant to me and you didn't even show up.
boundary_note: The statement focuses on the other person's failure, an accusation born of anger.

text: He just assumes I'll clean up his mess. I'm not his mother.
boundary_note: This is anger at being taken for granted and put into an unfair role.

text: I studied the wrong material because the syllabus was outdated. My grade is ruined.
boundary_note: The speaker is furious at the unfair consequences of someone else's mistake.

text: Another meeting that could have been an email. My whole morning is gone.
boundary_note: The emotion is anger directed at inefficiency and disrespect for one's time.

text: So after all that, you just decided my opinion didn't matter in the end.
boundary_note: Anger at being dismissed and invalidated, not sadness about the outcome.

text: They gave the promotion to him, after everything I did for this team. Unbelievable.
boundary_note: A sense of injustice and betrayal, not disappointment or personal failure.

text: I can't believe I fell for the same excuses again. I am so stupid.
boundary_note: Self-directed anger and frustration at one's own judgment, not self-pity.

text: Of course the one file I need is the only one that's corrupted. Just perfect.
boundary_note: Fury at the unfairness and terrible timing of the situation, not despair.

text: I guess all those promises you made meant absolutely nothing to you, then.
boundary_note: Rage over broken trust and perceived deception, not grief over a loss.

text: He didn't even look at me when he said it. Just completely dismissed me.
boundary_note: Anger at being treated as insignificant, not hurt feelings from being ignored.

text: Why do I even bother trying when I just end up looking like an idiot?
boundary_note: Frustration with oneself for repeated failure, not sadness about the failure itself.

text: My flight gets cancelled after I've already waited at the airport for six hours.
boundary_note: Exasperation and anger at the massive, unfair inconvenience, not simple dejection.

text: You couldn't even give me the courtesy of a simple phone call? Wow.
boundary_note: Fury at the disrespectful and cowardly action, not sadness from being left.

text: So they all went out together and conveniently forgot to invite me. That's cool.
boundary_note: Sarcastic anger at being intentionally excluded, not loneliness or sadness.

text: I should have known better than to trust anything they said to me.
boundary_note: Anger at personal gullibility, not sorrow over being deceived by others.

text: This whole system is a joke. I followed all the rules and still got penalized.
boundary_note: Rage at an unjust system, not despair over the personal negative consequence.

text: I waited all night for you and you didn't even bother to send a text.
boundary_note: Anger at the blatant disrespect for the speaker's time and feelings.

text: Five years of loyalty and they let me go in a five-minute email.
boundary_note: Fury over the impersonal and insulting dismissal, not sadness over losing a job.

text: Another promise I've broken to myself. I'm just pathetic at this point.
boundary_note: Intense self-loathing and anger at a lack of discipline, not depressive sadness.

text: The deadline is tomorrow and my computer just died. This cannot be happening.
boundary_note: Panicked anger at the absolute injustice of the timing, not helplessness.

text: So you're just throwing all of this away like it meant nothing?
boundary_note: Incredulous rage that shared history is being devalued, not just sorrow.

text: She just laughed it off when I told her how much it hurt me.
boundary_note: Anger at the invalidation and cruelty of the reaction, not the original hurt.

text: I let my guard down for one second, and this is what happens.
boundary_note: Frustration at one's own vulnerability being exploited, not regret for trusting.

text: Great, the power goes out right in the middle of the championship game.
boundary_note: Low-level anger at a frustrating interruption, not sadness about missing the game.

text: You took credit for my work. I have nothing else to say to you.
boundary_note: Cold fury over a deep personal and professional betrayal, not disappointment.

text: They left their mess for me to clean up again, as if I'm invisible.
boundary_note: Resentment at being taken for granted, not sadness about the extra work.

text: How could I have been so blind? All the signs were right there.
boundary_note: Rage at one's own lack of perception, not sadness about the truth.

text: I lost my keys again, on the one day I absolutely could not be late.
boundary_note: Frustration at fate and bad luck, not self-pity about being forgetful.

text: I'm not hurt, I'm just stunned you could be so thoughtless about this.
boundary_note: Disgusted anger at a lack of consideration, explicitly reframing away from sadness.

text: He knew how important today was, and he just decided not to show up.
boundary_note: Anger at a profound, deliberate act of disrespect and betrayal.

text: Wow, I am just the person everyone can walk all over, I guess.
boundary_note: Bitter, sarcastic anger at being perceived as a pushover, not resignation.

text: The policy changed without warning, making my entire project completely invalid now.
boundary_note: Rage at the unfairness of arbitrary rule changes, not sadness over wasted work.

text: All I asked for was a little honesty, and you couldn't even manage that.
boundary_note: Anger at the insulting dishonesty, not sadness from the lack of connection.

text: Apparently 'we'll call you' just means they threw my application in the trash.
boundary_note: Cynical anger at dishonest professional practices, not rejection sadness.

text: I hate that I let things get this far without saying anything about it.
boundary_note: Anger at one's own passivity, not regret over the current state of affairs.

text: This traffic is unbelievable. My appointment was twenty minutes ago. Just my luck.
boundary_note: Anger at external forces ruining a plan, not stress or sadness.

text: I told you that in confidence. I can't believe you'd just go and repeat it.
boundary_note: Fury over a significant betrayal of trust, not hurt feelings.

text: He borrowed the money months ago and now he's pretending he doesn't remember.
boundary_note: Indignation at the blatant disrespect and dishonesty, not sadness over lost money.

text: I gave them every single chance to be honest, and I still got played.
boundary_note: Anger at oneself for being too patient or naive, not just betrayal.

text: So the package was 'out for delivery' and now it's back at the warehouse.
boundary_note: Frustration with an illogical and failing system, not disappointment about the delay.

text: Don't tell me to 'calm down' when you're the one who broke your word.
boundary_note: Anger at being gaslit on top of the original offense.

text: She promised she would cover for me, and then just left me to handle it.
boundary_note: A feeling of being deliberately abandoned and betrayed, not just let down.

text: I spent weeks on that paper just for the professor to not even read it.
boundary_note: Rage that one's hard work was completely invalidated and disrespected.

text: I really thought I had finally figured it out. What a complete joke.
boundary_note: Self-directed anger at a perceived personal failure, not hopelessness.

text: After all this time, I guess my opinion just doesn't count for anything.
boundary_note: Anger at being invalidated and dismissed, not sadness from being left out.

text: You really just went ahead without me? Wow, okay. I see how it is.
boundary_note: Fury over betrayal and being deliberately excluded, not hurt feelings.

text: I can't believe I wasted so much energy on something that just fell apart.
boundary_note: Self-directed rage at a perceived personal failure, not grief over the loss.

text: So this is just how it ends? No explanation, no closure, nothing.
boundary_note: Outrage at the injustice and abruptness, not sorrow over the ending itself.

text: They promised they would be there. I guess some promises don't mean much.
boundary_note: White-hot anger at a broken promise and profound disrespect.

text: I suppose it was silly of me to expect you to remember what I said.
boundary_note: Passive-aggressive anger at being ignored, not sadness about being forgotten.

text: Why do I even bother trying when this is always the result?
boundary_note: Frustrated anger at one's own repeated, failed efforts, not despair.

text: Another rejection. I'm just so tired of being told I'm not good enough.
boundary_note: Building rage against an unfair system, not sadness from personal inadequacy.

text: He didn't even have the decency to tell me himself. I had to hear it from someone else.
boundary_note: Incensed by the cowardly and disrespectful method of communication.

text: Fine. Do whatever you want. It's clear you were going to anyway.
boundary_note: Resentful anger disguised as resignation; a challenge, not a surrender.

text: I poured my heart into that project and now I'm just... replaced.
boundary_note: Furious indignation at being devalued and discarded, not sorrow over the job loss.

text: Of course the one day I'm running late, this happens. Just perfect.
boundary_note: Hot frustration and anger at cosmic inconvenience, not sad resignation.

text: So that's it? You're just giving up on us after one argument?
boundary_note: Anger at perceived cowardice and lack of commitment, not sadness over the fight.

text: They all stood by and watched it happen. Not one of them said a thing.
boundary_note: Rage at the complicity and inaction of bystanders, not loneliness.

text: I really thought I had it this time. I feel so stupid.
boundary_note: Anger directed at oneself for failing or being naive, not dejection.

text: The system is just broken. There's nothing left to do, I guess.
boundary_note: Seething rage at systemic failure, masked as hopeless resignation.

text: Apparently, my years of loyalty to this company meant absolutely nothing.
boundary_note: Betrayed fury at being treated as disposable, not sadness about leaving.

text: Oh, so now you decide to show up? A little late for that, don't you think?
boundary_note: Bitter, sarcastic anger at someone's delayed and useless action.

text: I defended them, and this is the thanks I get. I'm such an idiot.
boundary_note: Furious at one's own misjudgment and misplaced loyalty.

text: I followed all the rules, did everything right, and still lost.
boundary_note: Outrage at an unjust outcome despite following the process perfectly.

text: I guess I just don't matter enough for a simple text back.
boundary_note: Anger at being blatantly ignored and disrespected, not loneliness.

text: Don't worry about me. I'll just figure it out on my own, like always.
boundary_note: Martyr-like anger, highlighting perceived neglect and forced self-reliance.

text: I should have known better than to get my hopes up again.
boundary_note: Anger at personal gullibility, not sadness about the disappointment.

text: Another fee for something that should have been included. I'm just so done.
boundary_note: Anger at being exploited by an unfair system, not weary sadness.

text: They all just laughed. I felt so small, standing there alone.
boundary_note: Humiliated rage at being mocked, not sorrow from isolation.

text: Was I not clear enough? I feel like I'm talking to a wall.
boundary_note: Intense frustration and anger at not being heard or understood.

text: It's my own fault for thinking things might actually change this time.
boundary_note: Self-directed anger for being foolishly optimistic, not despair.

text: The deadline was moved without any warning. Now everything is ruined.
boundary_note: Rage at the carelessness and chaos caused by an arbitrary decision.

text: He gave my idea to someone else. Just like that, it's not mine anymore.
boundary_note: Fury over intellectual theft and betrayal, not sadness over the loss.

text: I'm sorry my feelings are such an inconvenience for you.
boundary_note: Sarcastic anger aimed at someone being dismissive and invalidating.

text: I can't believe I let it get this bad. What was I thinking?
boundary_note: Anger and frustration at one's own inaction or poor choices.

text: This whole process has been a complete waste of my time and energy.
boundary_note: Anger at bureaucratic incompetence and inefficiency, not just fatigue.

text: She really said that in front of everyone? I'm just... speechless.
boundary_note: Stunned rage and indignation at a public betrayal or insult.

text: I guess I just expected you to have my back. My mistake.
boundary_note: Bitter anger over a perceived betrayal of loyalty, not just hurt feelings.

text: Of course I'm the last one to find out. Why am I not surprised?
boundary_note: Angry resignation at being consistently excluded, aimed inward and outward.

text: My flight is cancelled. After all that planning, I'm just stuck here.
boundary_note: Rage at the logistical failure and derailment of plans.

text: They took my word and twisted it into something ugly. I feel sick.
boundary_note: Violated anger at malicious misrepresentation, not sadness over misunderstanding.

text: Sure, blame me. It's always my fault anyway, isn't it?
boundary_note: Angry defiance against being made a scapegoat, not sad acceptance.

text: I just keep making the same mistakes over and over. I'm so tired.
boundary_note: Deep frustration and anger with oneself for a lack of progress.

text: So that's the final decision? It just doesn't make any sense at all.
boundary_note: Anger at an illogical and unjust ruling or outcome.

text: I can't believe they just left without saying a single word to me.
boundary_note: Focus is on the betrayal and disrespect, not the loneliness or loss.

text: Five years and they couldn't even bother to reply to my message.
boundary_note: Simmering anger at being dismissed, not dejection from being ignored.

text: Wow, so I just don't matter to anyone here, I guess.
boundary_note: Passive-aggressive accusation of neglect, masking fury at being made to feel invisible.

text: It's fine. I'll just handle everything myself, like I always do.
boundary_note: Resentment at being unsupported, not sadness about having a heavy burden.

text: So all that work was for nothing. That's just great.
boundary_note: Bitter sarcasm and fury over wasted effort, not disappointment in the outcome.

text: I really thought I could count on you for once.
boundary_note: Anger over a broken pattern of trust, not simple hurt feelings.

text: Why do I even try? It never makes a difference anyway.
boundary_note: Frustration and self-directed anger at repeated failure, not hopeless sadness.

text: He promised he'd be there. I guess I'm not that important.
boundary_note: Fury at a broken promise and perceived slight, not sadness over absence.

text: Of course this would happen to me, right before the deadline.
boundary_note: Anger at the universe's unfair timing, not sad resignation to fate.

text: I'm just so tired of being the only one who cares.
boundary_note: Exasperated anger at unequal emotional labor, not lonely sadness.

text: I guess my opinion doesn't have any value in this house.
boundary_note: Passive aggression fueled by anger at being invalidated and dismissed.

text: They gave the promotion to him? After everything I did?
boundary_note: Outrage at profound workplace injustice, not just personal disappointment.

text: I can't believe I fell for the same excuses again.
boundary_note: Self-directed fury for being naive, not sad regret for a mistake.

text: And the check bounced. I'm not even surprised at this point.
boundary_note: Cynical anger born of repeated frustration, not weary sadness.

text: You knew this was important to me, but you did it anyway.
boundary_note: Anger stemming from intentional disregard and disrespect, not simple hurt.

text: Another rejection letter. I guess my best just isn't good enough.
boundary_note: Frustration and anger at perceived inadequacy, not just dejection.

text: He just threw it away like it meant nothing to him.
boundary_note: Rage at the casual destruction of something meaningful, not grief over its loss.

text: Don't worry about me, I'll figure it out on my own.

text: The one day I don't bring an umbrella, it decides to pour.
boundary_note: Low-level anger at cosmic inconvenience, not sadness about being wet.

text: I feel so stupid for thinking things would be different this time.
boundary_note: Anger at one's own misplaced hope, not sadness that it failed.

text: She didn't even have the decency to tell me to my face.

text: So that's it? You're just walking away after everything we built?

text: My own family just stood there and let them say that.

text: All that studying, and the test covered none of it.

text: I guess I just have to accept being treated this way.

text: I'm always the one reaching out. I'm just done trying.

text: I pour my heart out and all you say is 'okay'?

text: The computer crashed and I lost hours of work. Of course.

text: Apparently, my loyalty to this company means absolutely nothing.

text: I should have known better than to trust a word he said.

text: No one ever told me the rules were different for me.

text: You watched me struggle and didn't lift a finger to help.

text: I'm just a convenience for you, aren't I?

text: I keep giving pieces of myself away for nothing in return.

text: This whole system is rigged. Why did I even bother playing?

text: They got away with it. There's just no justice in the world.

text: I guess I just have 'victim' written on my forehead.

text: My train is delayed again. I feel like I live at this station.

text: So you just told everyone my secret? Thanks, that's really helpful.
boundary_note: Sarcastic fury over a deep betrayal of confidence, not embarrassment.

text: I am so done being the bigger person all the time.

text: I guess after all my hard work, my contribution just doesn't mean anything to you.
boundary_note: Anger at being unappreciated, not sadness from failure.

text: He just took the credit for my entire project. I feel completely erased.

text: Why did I ever think I could trust them? I feel so incredibly stupid right now.

text: Of course the computer crashed right before I saved. All that work, just gone.

text: You promised you'd be there for me. It's just so disappointing to be left alone again.

text: They all went out and 'forgot' to invite me. I suppose I get the message.

text: I can't believe I missed the deadline. I had so much time and I just blew it.

text: After a six-hour delay, they cancelled the flight. My whole trip is just ruined.

text: It's fine, I'll just do it myself. It seems like I always have to anyway.

text: She didn't do any work but got the same grade. This whole system feels like a joke.

text: I shouldn't have said that. Now everyone thinks I'm a complete idiot.

text: I've been on hold for an hour. It feels like this company wants you to give up.

text: Wow, so you just make plans without even telling me? I feel so unimportant.

text: I lent him my favorite jacket and he returned it completely destroyed. No apology.

text: I knew this was a bad investment. Why do I never listen to my own gut feelings?

text: The one day I have a major interview, there's a huge accident. Of course.

text: I poured my heart into this and you barely even glanced at it. It's... disheartening.

text: They promised the promotion to me, then gave it to the manager's nephew. Unbelievable.

text: I can't believe I forgot their anniversary. I feel like such a terrible person.

text: The landlord raised the rent again. I work so hard and still can't get ahead.

text: Never mind, it's pretty clear you're not listening. I'll just stop talking.

text: That driver just cut across three lanes. It's like my safety means absolutely nothing.

text: I pushed away the one person who cared. It's all my fault and now I'm alone.

text: My luggage is gone. The airline just says 'sorry for the inconvenience'. That's it.

text: You said you'd pay me back. I guess I was just foolish to believe you.

text: I just found out what they were saying behind my back. It's just so cruel.

text: The opportunity was right there and I froze. I'm just so pathetic and cowardly.

text: Another rejection letter. I followed all the rules and it still wasn't good enough.

text: Don't worry about helping me. I shouldn't have asked in the first place.

text: The waiter has walked past our table ten times. I guess our money isn't good here.

text: I really let the team down with that presentation. I just can't forgive myself.

text: The leak ruined all my old photos. Decades of memories, just gone like that.

text: A simple 'sorry' doesn't fix this. You knew this would hurt me and you did it anyway.

text: My sister promised she'd help with Mom, but just disappeared. It's all on me now.

text: I should have gone to the doctor sooner. This pain is my own stupid fault.

text: The package was stolen right off my porch. I just feel so violated and helpless.

text: I really thought you were different. I guess I was wrong about you all along.

text: They just canceled the show we had tickets for. All that planning for nothing.

text: I can't believe I fell for that scam. How could I have been so naive?

text: After all this physical therapy, the doctor says there's no improvement. What a waste.

text: Why do I even bother trying anymore? The result is always the same failure.

text: Fine, just go. It’s what everyone does eventually, why would you be different?

text: What was the point of all that effort? It all just fell apart anyway.
boundary_note: Sounds like anger at failure, but is really despair over the futility of effort.

text: They all act like it's fine. Am I the only one who's completely broken by this?
boundary_note: Accusatory language stems from a feeling of isolation and being misunderstood in grief.

text: I should have known better than to get my hopes up. Of course it ended this way.
boundary_note: Self-blame is a symptom of resignation and deep disappointment, not anger.

text: You promised it would be different this time. Why did I ever believe you?
boundary_note: The accusation is rooted in the pain of betrayal, not pure rage.

text: This whole system is rigged. You can't win no matter how hard you fight.
boundary_note: Expresses a sense of utter powerlessness and systemic defeat, not just anger.

text: It's hilarious how easily they all moved on. Guess I was the only one who cared.
boundary_note: The sarcasm is a coping mechanism for feeling left behind and insignificant.

text: Another brilliant mistake by me. I am just incapable of getting anything right.
boundary_note: The self-directed frustration comes from a place of learned helplessness and despair.

text: Don't bother with excuses. Your actions made it perfectly clear how you feel.
boundary_note: The curt dismissal is a defense against further hurt, driven by sadness.

text: Another rejection. I don't know why I expected anything different this time around.
boundary_note: Apparent bitterness is actually resignation and a crushed sense of hope.

text: Nobody ever actually listens. I might as well be talking to a brick wall.
boundary_note: Frustrated words that actually convey deep loneliness and feeling invisible to others.

text: I've ruined it all again. There's just something fundamentally wrong with me.
boundary_note: This is not anger at a mistake, but despair about a perceived character flaw.

text: Was I just a complete fool for trusting you with everything?
boundary_note: The accusatory question is a reflection of heartbreak and shattered trust.

text: Great, the one thing I was looking forward to is cancelled. Of course it is.
boundary_note: Sarcasm here indicates a pattern of disappointment and a lack of hope.

text: They have no idea what it's like. It's so easy for them to judge from the outside.
boundary_note: The resentment comes from a place of isolation in one's own suffering.

text: I am so tired of making the same mistakes over and over. I'll never learn.
boundary_note: Exhaustion and hopelessness, not anger, are the core emotions behind this self-criticism.

text: I gave you everything I had, and you just threw it away like it was nothing.
boundary_note: The accusation highlights the speaker's feeling of being devalued and heartbroken.

text: This is hopeless. Every door I try to open gets slammed in my face.
boundary_note: The frustration is a symptom of complete and utter despair about one's prospects.

text: They all have their perfect lives. Why can't I just have a moment of that?
boundary_note: This is not jealousy-anger, but a sad longing for a different, happier life.

text: Why do I even try to connect with people? I always just end up getting hurt.
boundary_note: The frustrated question expresses a painful pattern of rejection and withdrawal.

text: So that's it then? After everything, it just ends like this? Unbelievable.
boundary_note: The disbelief and frustration mask a deep sense of loss and grief.

text: Go on, tell me again how this is my fault. I'm used to hearing it.
boundary_note: The challenging tone is a sign of resignation and weariness from repeated blame.

text: I'm always the one left behind. No one ever stays for me.
boundary_note: This is a statement of painful, recurring abandonment, not an angry accusation.

text: I'm so stupid for thinking things could actually get better for once.
boundary_note: The self-insult is about the pain of disappointed hope, not anger at a mistake.

text: This is just my luck, isn't it? Nothing can ever just go right for me.
boundary_note: The cynical, bitter tone reflects a deep-seated belief that they are fated to fail.

text: You never even asked my side of it. You just decided I was the bad guy.
boundary_note: The accusation is about the sadness of being fundamentally misunderstood and unheard.

text: They'll forget this in a week. For me, it's my entire world crumbling down.
boundary_note: Seems like resentment but is really about the intense loneliness of one's own grief.

text: I try so hard to be enough, but it's never enough. I always fall short.
boundary_note: This isn't frustration with a task, but despair about one's inherent worth.

text: I'm just so tired of fighting this battle every single day. It never gets easier.
boundary_note: The statement is one of profound weariness and hopelessness, not combative anger.

text: Just leave me alone. I'm sure you have better things to do anyway.
boundary_note: The push-away is driven by a sad belief of being a burden.

text: Of course they picked him. People like me are just meant to be background characters.
boundary_note: The bitter comparison reveals feelings of invisibility and low self-worth, not anger.

text: There's no point in explaining. No one ever understands what I mean anyway.
boundary_note: This statement of futility comes from a history of being misunderstood, causing sadness.

text: I knew this would happen. I always manage to drive everyone away in the end.
boundary_note: This is not self-anger, but a sad, self-fulfilling prophecy of loneliness.

text: I can't even look at you right now. It hurts too much to see you.
boundary_note: This sounds like angry avoidance, but the reason given is deep emotional pain.

text: They all seem so happy and normal. What is so wrong with my life?
boundary_note: A bitter-sounding comparison that reveals a deep, sad sense of personal alienation.

text: I'm done. I can't keep pouring my heart into things that just break apart.
boundary_note: The declaration of 'being done' comes from emotional exhaustion and defeat.

text: Why do I let myself care so much? It's my own fault for being this way.
boundary_note: Blaming a personality trait is a sad expression of wanting to stop feeling hurt.

text: They moved on so fast. It's like the time we spent together meant nothing at all.
boundary_note: The core emotion is the sadness of feeling that shared memories were meaningless.

text: You talk about the future, but I don't see how I fit in it anymore.
boundary_note: A seemingly confrontational statement that is actually expressing a sad sense of exclusion.

text: I worked so hard for nothing. What's the point in even trying anymore?
boundary_note: This is resignation and hopelessness, not anger at the lack of results.

text: You promised it would be different. I can't believe I was stupid enough to listen.
boundary_note: The focus is on personal heartbreak and feeling foolish, not rage.

text: Great, another thing I've completely ruined. Why am I even surprised at this point?
boundary_note: This is self-directed despair and a feeling of worthlessness, not frustration.

text: They all moved on so fast. I guess I never really mattered to them anyway.
boundary_note: Expresses a deep sense of loneliness and being forgotten, not bitterness.

text: Of course it didn't work out. Nothing ever does for me, it's just my luck.
boundary_note: This shows fatalism and defeat, not anger at the specific circumstance.

text: I'm so sick of my own weakness. I just manage to sabotage everything good.
boundary_note: This is profound self-loathing and grief over personal flaws, not simple frustration.

text: Why do you bother with fake apologies? We both know you don't mean it.
boundary_note: The tone is weary and defeated, not accusatory or vengeful.

text: Everyone else gets it right so easily. What's so fundamentally broken about me?
boundary_note: The comparison leads to despair and a feeling of inadequacy, not jealousy or anger.

text: I really thought this time would be the one. What a complete and utter fool.
boundary_note: The core emotion is deep disappointment in oneself and shattered hope.

text: It doesn't matter what I do, the outcome is always the same. I'm just done.
boundary_note: This is the language of burnout and total surrender, not a fight.

text: I gave you all of me. And you threw it away like it was nothing.
boundary_note: The accusation stems from deep hurt and a sense of loss, not fury.

text: They don't even notice I'm gone. It's like I was never really there.
boundary_note: A painful realization of invisibility and loneliness, not anger at being ignored.

text: Another rejection. I should just accept I'm never going to be good enough.
boundary_note: This is a statement of accepted defeat, not anger about being rejected.

text: I'm just a burden to everyone around me. Why do I even try to connect?
boundary_note: The root is a painful sense of worthlessness and isolation.

text: Don't say you understand. You have no idea what it's like to feel this broken.
boundary_note: This is a plea born from deep loneliness and a feeling of being misunderstood.

text: It's fine, I'm used to being forgotten. Everyone leaves in the end, don't they?
boundary_note: The cynical tone masks a deep-seated sadness about abandonment.

text: This whole thing was a colossal waste of my time. It all meant nothing.
boundary_note: The statement mourns the loss of effort and hope, not rage at the failure.

text: I try so hard to be better, but I always end up back here. Helpless.
boundary_note: This is about the despair of a repeating cycle of personal failure.

text: Was it all just a lie? Was any of it real to you at all?
boundary_note: These questions come from a place of profound heartbreak and confusion, not accusation.

text: They're probably all laughing at me behind my back. I wouldn't blame them.
boundary_note: The paranoia is rooted in social anxiety and low self-worth, not anger.

text: I can't believe I let myself hope again. It just makes the fall hurt more.
boundary_note: This is grief over lost hope and self-reproach for being vulnerable.

text: Another bill I can't pay. What's the point of this endless, losing struggle?
boundary_note: Expresses overwhelming despair and the feeling of being trapped, not financial frustration.

text: You look right through me. Do you even remember I exist when I'm not here?
boundary_note: The accusation is a cry of pain from feeling invisible and unimportant.

text: Of course they gave the promotion to him. I'm just part of the furniture here.
boundary_note: A statement of resignation and feeling unseen, not anger at the colleague.

text: Why do I even open my mouth? I just end up sounding like an idiot.
boundary_note: This is self-criticism born from social anxiety and regret, not momentary frustration.

text: Just my luck. The one day I felt okay, this has to happen. Unbelievable.
boundary_note: The disbelief is tinged with weary defeat, not energetic anger at fate.

text: Go have fun. Don't worry about me, I'm used to being left behind.
boundary_note: This passive-aggressive statement masks a deep sadness of being excluded.

text: They won't miss me. Nobody ever does. It's better this way for them.
boundary_note: This is a conclusion of utter worthlessness and self-isolation, a sign of depression.

text: I'm so tired of fighting this battle in my head every single day. I'm losing.
boundary_note: This speaks to profound mental exhaustion and a sense of impending defeat.

text: The test results are in. Figures. Some things are just broken beyond repair.
boundary_note: The tone is one of finality and the grief of accepting a terrible outcome.

text: You say that now, but your actions show what you really think. It's fine.
boundary_note: The 'it's fine' signals resignation and the pain of broken trust, not anger.

text: Why does everyone else get a happy ending? What did I do to deserve this?
boundary_note: This is a mournful, philosophical question about fairness, not a jealous complaint.

text: It's my own fault for getting attached. I always make the same stupid mistakes.
boundary_note: The speaker is grieving their own perceived flaws and repeated patterns of pain.

text: I did everything right. I followed all the rules. And for what? This mockery.
boundary_note: The bitterness comes from a place of deep disappointment and shattered belief.

text: Just tell me the truth. Was I ever more than a convenience for you?
boundary_note: This question seeks to confirm a painful suspicion, bracing for more hurt.

text: They look so happy. They have no idea what it feels like to be this empty.
boundary_note: A statement of profound isolation and alienation, not envy or bitterness.

text: I'm the problem, aren't I? I'm the reason everything falls apart eventually.
boundary_note: A rhetorical question that expresses deep-seated guilt and self-blame.

text: What a joke. All that effort, all that hope, all for this predictable ending.
boundary_note: The cynicism is a defense mechanism against overwhelming disappointment.

text: Is this what you wanted? To see me completely and utterly broken? Congratulations.
boundary_note: The sarcasm masks a profound sense of betrayal and personal devastation.

text: No one checked in. I could disappear and they wouldn't even notice for weeks.
boundary_note: A statement of fact colored by extreme loneliness and a feeling of invisibility.

text: Why do I even bother trying? I just break everything I'm given.
boundary_note: This is a statement of resignation and hopelessness, not self-directed anger.

text: Another failure. I'm just not built for this world, and I never will be.
boundary_note: The tone is one of profound despair and giving up, not frustration.

text: I should've known better. I ruin every good thing, it's just what I do.
boundary_note: The bitterness masks a deep-seated belief in one's own inadequacy and sorrow.

text: Fine, I'll handle it myself. It's clear I'm not worth anyone's time anyway.
boundary_note: This isn't an angry declaration of independence; it's a statement of perceived worthlessness.

text: I hate what I've become. All that work for absolutely nothing to show for it.
boundary_note: The 'hate' is directed at the failure and loss, stemming from grief, not rage.

text: I can't believe I said that. Now everyone will see how pathetic I really am.
boundary_note: Focuses on shame and the fear of being seen as inadequate, not anger.

text: I'm my own worst enemy. There's no point in expecting anything good anymore.
boundary_note: This is an expression of complete fatalism and defeat, not a desire to change.

text: I had one simple task, and I completely blew it. Of course, I did.
boundary_note: The sarcasm is a cover for deep disappointment and reinforces a negative self-view.

text: This is all my fault. I'm the reason everything fell apart for us.
boundary_note: This is an expression of crushing guilt and sorrow, not self-flagellation from anger.

text: What is wrong with me? I push away anyone who ever tries to get close.
boundary_note: The question is a cry of despair over a painful pattern, not angry self-interrogation.

text: You promised you would be here. I can't believe I was stupid enough to trust you.
boundary_note: The accusation stems from the deep hurt of betrayal, not a desire to attack.

text: Was I just a complete joke to you this entire time? Just tell me.
boundary_note: The question comes from a place of shattered self-worth and pain, not aggression.

text: Don't you dare say you understand. You have no idea what your actions cost me.
boundary_note: This is pushing someone away out of profound hurt, not attacking them in anger.

text: After everything I did for us, you just walk away without a single word.
boundary_note: Focuses on the pain of abandonment and the futility of past efforts, not rage.

text: Why did you have to give me so much hope? It only made this fall hurt more.
boundary_note: The blame is a projection of grief over what was lost and what could have been.

text: I don't even know who you are anymore. The person I loved would never do this.
boundary_note: This is a statement of disillusionment and mourning for the person they thought they knew.

text: Just leave then! It’s what everyone does eventually. Why would you be any different?
boundary_note: A bitter, preemptive strike born from a deep-seated fear and expectation of abandonment.

text: All that talk about forever, and you couldn't even make it through the year.
boundary_note: The sarcastic tone highlights the profound disappointment in a broken promise, not fury.

text: So that's it? You're just giving up on everything we built together?
boundary_note: The accusatory question is rooted in disbelief and the sorrow of impending loss.

text: I guess I was just a temporary stop for you. How could I have missed that?
boundary_note: The bitterness is directed inward as much as outward, a sign of hurt ego and sadness.

text: They all get to be happy so easily. Why is it always a fight for me?
boundary_note: The comparison highlights personal despair and exhaustion, not animosity toward others.

text: Go have your fun. I'm sure no one will even notice that I'm gone.
boundary_note: This sounds dismissive but is an expression of deep social isolation and feeling invisible.

text: Must be nice to have it all figured out while the rest of us are drowning.
boundary_note: The bitter tone stems from a feeling of being left behind, not from true resentment.

text: I am so sick of hearing about their perfect lives. It's just a constant reminder.
boundary_note: This isn't anger at them, but sadness triggered by comparison and what one lacks.

text: Of course they chose him for the job. Why did I even think I had a chance?
boundary_note: The comment is less about the other person and more about confirming personal inadequacy.

text: Everyone else just gets to move on, but I'm stuck here with all this damage.
boundary_note: Expresses a profound sense of loneliness and injustice in suffering, not anger at others.

text: Just stop trying to help. Nobody understands and they only make it worse.
boundary_note: This is lashing out from a place of isolation and the pain of being misunderstood.

text: I see them with their happy families and it just feels like a knife twisting.
boundary_note: The violent imagery describes the intensity of grief, not animosity towards those families.

text: Why does everyone find their person so easily? It feels like a cosmic joke.
boundary_note: The question is directed at the universe, expressing loneliness and romantic despair.

text: They have no idea how much I sacrificed. To them, it's just another failure.
boundary_note: The core emotion is the pain of being unseen and unappreciated, not anger.

text: What is the point of all this? The world just grinds you down eventually.
boundary_note: This isn't a challenge; it's a statement of complete and utter existential defeat.

text: I pour my soul into my work, and for what? The outcome is always the same.
boundary_note: The frustration is rooted in futility and exhaustion, not a desire to fight.

text: This is just my luck. One step forward, two steps back. Every single time.
boundary_note: Expresses weary resignation to a perceived pattern of failure, not active anger.

text: The rules are rigged. Good things don't happen to people like me in this life.
boundary_note: This is a conclusion of despair, a belief that effort is pointless.

text: This whole system is just designed to watch you drown slowly. There's no escape.
boundary_note: The imagery conveys a sense of being trapped and hopeless, not rebellious anger.

text: I followed every single step, and it still fell apart. Why does nothing work?
boundary_note: The question is a lament of defeat, not an angry demand for an answer.

text: This illness will never leave me alone, will it? It just keeps on taking.
boundary_note: The language personifies the illness as a thief, focusing on continuous, wearying loss.

text: So this is it? All that struggle just to end up right back at square one.
boundary_note: The question expresses the sorrow of wasted effort and a cyclical, inescapable failure.

text: No matter who wins, nothing ever really changes for us. It's all a pointless game.
boundary_note: This is political disillusionment born of hopelessness, not angry protest.

text: The one day I feel a glimmer of hope, the universe smacks me down.
boundary_note: The tone is one of bitter resignation to a perceived cruel fate, not rage.

text: Great, I've managed to ruin another perfectly good thing. Just like always.
boundary_note: Phrase 'just like always' indicates resigned despair, not temporary anger.

text: Why couldn't you just have been honest with me from the start?
boundary_note: The question stems from hurt and betrayal, not aggressive accusation.

text: Of course it's raining today. Why would anything ever go my way?
boundary_note: This is fatalism and despair about life, not anger at the weather.

text: Why do they get everything so easily? They don't even have to try.
boundary_note: Comparison leads to sadness about one's own struggles, not anger at others' success.

text: What is wrong with me? Why can't I ever get anything right?
boundary_note: The question is rhetorical, expressing deep self-doubt and hopelessness.

text: I gave you everything, and for what? You just threw it all away.
boundary_note: Focuses on the pain of wasted love and effort, which is heartbreak.

text: This is just my luck. Another dead end, no matter which path I take.
boundary_note: Expresses a deep sense of hopelessness and being trapped by fate.

text: Everyone just moves on and forgets. Am I the only one still stuck here?
boundary_note: This expresses feelings of isolation in grief, not anger at others' happiness.

text: I'm so tired of trying. I just end up failing every single time.
boundary_note: The core emotion is exhaustion and a sense of complete defeat.

text: Do you have any idea how much that hurt? Or do you just not care?
boundary_note: This is a cry of pain, feeling unheard and abandoned.

text: This whole system is rigged. There's just no point in even trying to win.
boundary_note: The core feeling is resignation and the grief of powerlessness.

text: They all said they'd support me, but where are they now? It was all just words.
boundary_note: This is sadness and hurt from feeling abandoned by a group.

text: Another missed opportunity. I guess I'm just not cut out for this.
boundary_note: This is resignation and self-directed grief, not frustration at the task.

text: You promised you'd be there. You always make promises you can't keep.
boundary_note: Frustration comes from a place of recurring disappointment and sadness.

text: Nothing ever changes. It's just the same horrible cycle over and over again.
boundary_note: Highlights despair over a static, negative situation, not just frustration.

text: I'm tired of people making promises they have no intention of keeping.
boundary_note: The 'tiredness' is emotional exhaustion from repeated disappointment.

text: I guess this is just who I am. Someone who messes everything up.
boundary_note: A statement of sad acceptance about a perceived permanent flaw.

text: Just leave me alone. I can't even look at you right now.
boundary_note: The withdrawal is a reaction to deep hurt, not a desire to attack.

text: Great, another bill I can't pay. This hole just keeps getting deeper.
boundary_note: Sarcasm hides overwhelming sadness and hopelessness about a financial trap.

text: They'll never understand what this feels like. They don't even try to.
boundary_note: This is about the profound loneliness and sadness of being misunderstood.

text: Why do I even bother setting goals? I know I'll never reach them.
boundary_note: Expresses profound futility and despair, not simple annoyance.

text: Why do you always have to say that? Don't you see I'm breaking?
boundary_note: A plea for empathy, stemming from immense vulnerability and sadness.

text: Here we go again. I knew it was too good to be true for long.
boundary_note: This is learned helplessness and sadness from expecting disappointment.

text: It's always someone else who gets the promotion. I'm just invisible.
boundary_note: The core feeling is dejection and sadness from being consistently overlooked.

text: Of course I said the wrong thing. It's what I always do.
boundary_note: The tone is one of defeated expectation, a form of self-grief.

text: You've made your choice. I guess I was never enough for you.
boundary_note: This is sad acceptance of rejection and perceived inadequacy.

text: I am so done with this. The universe can just stop now.
boundary_note: This is a plea from exhaustion and defeat, not an aggressive demand.

text: Why does everyone else seem so happy? What am I doing wrong?
boundary_note: This is a cry of isolation and sadness, not jealousy-fueled anger.

text: I look at my life and just think, 'Is this really all there is?'
boundary_note: This is existential sadness and disappointment, not outward anger.

text: I don't even know who you are anymore. When did you change?
boundary_note: This expresses grief over the loss of a person and a relationship.

text: What's the point of planning? Something always comes along to ruin it.
boundary_note: Speaks to a sense of futility and deep disappointment, not momentary rage.

text: Look at them, celebrating. They have no idea what they've taken from us.
boundary_note: This expresses a sense of powerlessness and deep grief, not vengeful anger.

text: I'm my own worst enemy. I always sabotage any chance I get.
boundary_note: Focus is on a pattern of self-destruction, which is a source of grief.

text: Must be nice to just move on, leaving this mess for me.
boundary_note: Bitterness masks a feeling of being abandoned and overwhelmed with sorrow.

text: This isn't fair. I did everything right and it still fell apart.
boundary_note: The cry of 'not fair' is about grief over an unjust outcome.

text: I can't believe they chose him after all I did for them.
boundary_note: The disbelief comes from hurt and a sense of deep personal betrayal.

text: There's no point. I'm just not good enough and I never will be.
boundary_note: This is a statement of complete hopelessness and deep-seated sadness.

text: Was it really too much to ask for you to just try?
boundary_note: Despair over a fundamental, unmet need in a relationship, not just annoyance.

text: I hate this. I just want it all to stop for a while.
boundary_note: Expresses a desire to escape from overwhelming sorrow, not fight it.

text: People just take and take until there's nothing left. It's always the same.
boundary_note: Cynicism born from repeated hurt, expressing world-weary sadness.

text: Why do you always have to twist the knife? You know exactly what hurts.
boundary_note: Focuses on the pain of being hurt by a loved one, not on retaliation.

text: Great, I messed it up again. I don't know why I even try anymore.
boundary_note: The tone is one of resignation and hopelessness, not self-directed rage.

text: I guess everyone moves on eventually. I'm always the one left behind.
boundary_note: A statement of painful loneliness and abandonment, not bitterness toward others' success.

text: So this is it. All that work for absolutely nothing. Just fantastic.
boundary_note: Sarcasm masks deep disappointment and a sense of utter futility.

text: You promised you'd be there. I can't believe I was foolish enough to trust you.
boundary_note: This is about the heartbreak of a broken promise, not a desire for confrontation.

text: I'm the only one who ever seems to remember. Does it mean nothing to anyone else?
boundary_note: Expresses the sorrow of feeling isolated in one's own memories and feelings.

text: Of course I failed. It's just what I do. It was stupid to hope otherwise.
boundary_note: This is deep-seated despair and self-disappointment, not motivational frustration.

text: Another rejection. The universe is really making its opinion of me clear.
boundary_note: Personifies the situation to express a feeling of being cosmically unwanted and defeated.

text: Just leave me alone. I'm tired of you pretending to care about this.
boundary_note: A defensive plea for isolation stemming from pain, not aggressive rejection.

text: I can't seem to do anything right. I just make everything worse for everyone.
boundary_note: Expresses a painful sense of being a burden, which is a form of grief.

text: Must be nice to have it so easy. They'll never know what this feels like.
boundary_note: The core emotion is the sadness of being misunderstood and alone in struggle.

text: This whole thing is just pointless. Why are we even fighting to keep it going?
boundary_note: A question born from exhaustion and hopelessness, not a challenge.

text: Was it all a lie? I don't even know what was real with you anymore.
boundary_note: Expresses the profound sorrow of betrayal and shattered reality, not suspicion.

text: I don't belong here. Everyone can see it, they just don't say anything.
boundary_note: This is about the deep sadness of social isolation and feeling like an outcast.

text: There's something wrong with me. Why can't I just be happy like a normal person?
boundary_note: A cry of despair about one's own perceived brokenness, not self-hatred.

text: Of course it broke. Nothing good is ever allowed to last, is it?
boundary_note: A rhetorical question expressing deep pessimism and resignation to loss.

text: Don't touch me. Your pity is the last thing I could possibly want right now.
boundary_note: Defensive language used to protect a deep, sorrowful vulnerability.

text: I'm so tired of trying. What's the point if I always end up here?
boundary_note: This is the language of giving up, a hallmark of profound sadness and fatigue.

text: They all just talk over me. It's like my voice doesn't even exist.
boundary_note: Focuses on the pain of being invisible and unheard, not on the rudeness of others.

text: Another bill I can't pay. This hole just keeps getting deeper and deeper.
boundary_note: Uses the language of frustration to describe a state of overwhelming despair.

text: Is this what you wanted? To see me completely fall apart? Congratulations.
boundary_note: Sarcastic accusation that covers a feeling of utter defeat and heartbreak.

text: I hate the person I've become. I just want to feel like myself again.
boundary_note: The core is grief for a past self, not anger at the present one.

text: Why do people make promises they have no intention of keeping? It's just cruel.
boundary_note: A philosophical question born from personal pain and disillusionment, not rage at liars.

text: Well, the test results are in. Just as I thought. It's over.
boundary_note: The tone is flat and resigned, indicating a complete loss of hope.

text: I gave you everything I had. And you just threw it away like trash.
boundary_note: The focus is on the profound sense of loss and feeling devalued.

text: Another birthday alone. I guess some people just aren't meant to be remembered.
boundary_note: The accusatory tone is aimed at fate itself, expressing deep loneliness.

text: What is wrong with my brain? Why can't I remember the simplest things?
boundary_note: A cry of despair over a perceived personal failing, not frustration.

text: They don't get it. They just say 'cheer up' like it's a switch.
boundary_note: This highlights the isolating pain of being misunderstood by those who try to help.

text: Stop telling me it will be okay. You don't know that. You're just talking.
boundary_note: Lashing out against false hope, which deepens the feeling of despair.

text: The one thing I asked for. The one thing. And it was still too much.
boundary_note: Repetition emphasizes the depth of disappointment, not the anger of being denied.

text: I'm just a burden, aren't I? I ruin everything I'm a part of.
boundary_note: A deeply sorrowful belief about one's own negative impact on the world.

text: No one ever stays. What's the point of even letting people in anymore?
boundary_note: A rhetorical question that shows a deep-seated fear of abandonment and sorrow.

text: I can't even look at you right now. The disappointment is just too much.
boundary_note: The speaker is overwhelmed by hurt and sorrow, not by anger or a desire to fight.

text: It just never stops. One bad thing happens right after the other.
boundary_note: Expresses a sense of being relentlessly beaten down by life, leading to despair.

text: Why do I even open my mouth? I always say the wrong thing anyway.
boundary_note: This is not self-criticism to improve, but a statement of resigned failure.

text: They've already forgotten all about it. For me, it feels like yesterday.
boundary_note: Focuses on the loneliness of grieving while others have moved on.

text: Whatever. Do what you want. It clearly doesn't matter what I think.
boundary_note: A statement of complete surrender and emotional withdrawal, not a challenge.

text: Another sleepless night. My own mind won't even give me a moment of peace.
boundary_note: Frames an internal struggle as an attack, but the emotion is weariness and despair.

text: I hate this city. It's just full of ghosts and memories I can't escape.
boundary_note: Anger at the place is a proxy for the grief and loss it represents.

text: They all have their perfect lives. What am I doing so wrong all the time?
boundary_note: Comparison leads to a sense of personal failure and sorrow, not jealousy or rage.
